channel.title: Loom on MetaLoom
channel.description: Recent content in Loom on MetaLoom

item.title: Artifacts
item.description: Loom publishes JVM artifacts and container deployment assets separately. Maven Artifacts See Maven Artifacts for the BOM and the primary client and shared model modules.

item.title: Authentication
item.description: Loom uses JWT (JSON Web Token) bearer tokens for API authentication. OAuth2 integration is also available.

item.title: Configuration
item.description: Loom is configured via a YAML file (loom.yml) with optional environment variable overrides. Config File Locations Loom looks for loom.

item.title: Containers
item.description: The Loom server is distributed as a Docker image. Running with Docker docker run -d \ --name loom \ -p 8092:8092 \ -p 8091:8091 \ -p 8989:8989 \ -e LOOM_DB_HOST=postgres \ -e LOOM_DB_PASSWORD=secret \ -e LOOM_INITIAL_PASSWORD=admin123 \ -v loom-config:/app/config \ metaloom/loom:latest Docker Compose version: "3.

item.title: Examples
item.description: The /examples directory of the source repository contains runnable example projects. Listing Examples The examples/ Maven module contains sub-projects for both Loom and Cortex use cases.

item.title: Features
item.description: Loom provides a comprehensive set of features for managing digital media assets and their associated metadata.

item.title: Helm Chart
item.description: The Loom Helm chart is the Kubernetes deployment entry point for running the server in-cluster.

item.title: Java Client Usage
item.description: The loom-rest-client module provides a typed HTTP client for JVM applications. Maven Dependency <dependency> <groupId>io.

item.title: Maven Artifacts
item.description: All Loom artifacts are published under the io.metaloom group. BOM POM Use the BOM to align versions across Loom and Cortex modules:

item.title: REST API
item.description: Loom exposes its full functionality via a versioned REST API. All endpoints are rooted at /api/v1.
